WordPress.Is.Dead

  • The Proof
Receipts, kept honest

The old way.
The new way.

The most charitable side-by-side we could write while still telling the truth. WordPress is the column on the left.

WordPress 2003–2026

  • Buy domain, buy hosting, install LAMP stack
  • Run 5-minute install (it is not 5 minutes)
  • Pick a theme, edit child theme, lose child theme on update
  • Install 14 plugins to do what the theme should have done
  • One of the plugins was abandoned in 2019
  • Disable XML-RPC because of brute force traffic
  • Install a security plugin, then a caching plugin, then a plugin to manage the other plugins
  • Get the white screen of death after a minor update
  • SSH in, rename the plugins folder, get back to admin
  • Repeat every six weeks for the rest of your life

The Prompt 2026–

  • Open Claude Code in a folder
  • Type one sentence describing the site
  • Receive HTML, CSS, and copy
  • Drag the folder onto a static host
  • Done
  • Want a new page? Type one more sentence.
  • Want to change the colors? Type one more sentence.
  • Want a blog? It is just more HTML files.
  • There is nothing to update.
  • There is nothing to hack.
~60s
Time to ship this site
0
Plugins installed
0
Database queries
$0
Annual theme renewals
A representative bug report from the old world

"The site is down because Jetpack updated."

You will never have to send this Slack message again. Not because Jetpack got better, but because Jetpack is no longer load-bearing in your professional life.

Pay your respects →
